mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 300 in the CPIAUCSL series triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as bond yields rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 despite tightening signals.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signal a tightening labor market that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns with Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS combined with declining HOUST indicates weak consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle recessions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS rises and HOUST falls during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals expanding industrial production, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate profits rise.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain negative despite INDPRO growth in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth in GDPC1 indicates robust economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investor confidence rises.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in UNRATE below 3.5% signals labor market overheating, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to inflationary pressures.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% triggers risk-off sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as borrowing costs rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.
